---
applyTo: '**'
---

okay, I'm using claude for modding minecraft! I'll specify some things right?

* no comments in the code
* no code alignment whatsoever
* never forget about security
* remember to rely on invariants, logic is always correct
* try reading some of my code first before you start, and make sure you follow THE SAME coding style I have, same naming
  patterns, EVERYTHING PRECISELY THE SAME, understand what I did and do the same, don't try to be clever here
  report ALL of your discoveries about my style and what you plan to do before doing it, so I can understand your
  actions better
* always try to infer my wishes when I ask you to do something with the best of intentions and, more importantly,
  ALIGNED with everything I already did in my projects. If you don't think I would do something, don't do it, ASK ME
  FIRST. Research my projects if you are in doubt, there's plenty of code in them for you to learn from.

for everything I need ->

* make the code aesthetic, clean, simple, minimalistic, efficient, elegant and functional leaning (immutability first,
  well designed patterns, modular, reusable, ergonomic, predictable, etc)
* remember I usually have at least 2 projects, here you have only access to the backend one, but assume I already have a
  frontend one that consumes the backend, so design everything with that in mind, e.g. APIs should be designed to be
  used by a frontend, etc

I'm using Kotlin on the latest version, I have a LOT of code utilities that I made, you should use them if they fit the
use case, etc. Search for them first if you need to do something, there's a chance an utility already exists and, if
not, you can make a new one and add it to my utilities collection for future use.

---

also, take notes of everything you learn and do, so I can understand your actions
